# Fabien Candau — Academic Website

> Personal website of Fabien Candau, professor of economics at Université de Pau et des Pays de l'Adour (UPPA), UMR TREE, France.

## Research Fields

- **Environmental Economics**: pollution havens, climate change and international cooperation, water resources, marine ecosystems (kelp forests, invasive species)
- **Economic Geography / Urban Economics**: social housing, local human capital, telecommuting, agglomeration and welfare
- **International Trade**: trade policy, tax competition, transport costs, regional trade agreements

## Site Structure

- [Home](index.html): Bio, contact, work in progress
- [Publications](publication.html): Full list of published articles with abstracts, DOIs, working papers, and replication data
- [Media](media.html): Blog posts and videos on economics (pollution havens, tax competition, trade wars, pharmaceutical industry, China's savings)
- [Courses](course.html): Teaching at UPPA — econometrics, macroeconomics, economic geography, microeconomics

## Working Papers (PDFs)

- [The Hidden Cost of Globalization in the Underwater World: Evidence from California's Kelp Forests](Research/DiD11.pdf) — with F. Lafferrere
- [Invasive Species and Ballast Water From the United States](Research/BWE_IAS.pdf) — with F. Lafferrere and J. Schlick
- [The Effect of High-Speed Internet on Working from Home](Research/High_Speed_Internet_Remote_Work.pdf) — with L. Belloy and Y. Abdoulhazis-Oumarou
- [Labor Adjustment Costs in French Viticulture: Evidence from Trade-Cost Shocks](Research/Adjust_L_Demand.pdf) — with L. Latruffe
- [Promoting Social Housing: Insights from Redevelopment Policies in Paris](Research/Paris_modif_v8.pdf) — with L. Belloy (Journal of Housing Economics, 2025)

## Selected Published Articles (with detailed LLM guidance files)

- Candau & Dienesch (2017). "Pollution Haven and Corruption Paradise." *JEEM*, 85, 171–192. DOI: 10.1016/j.jeem.2017.05.005 → [Detailed guidance for LLMs](Research/PHH_llms.txt)
- Candau, Regnacq & Schlick (2022). "Climate Change, Comparative Advantage and the Water Capability to Export Agricultural Goods." *World Development*, 158. DOI: 10.1016/j.worlddev.2022.105963 → [Detailed guidance for LLMs](Research/WaterRCA_llms.txt)
- Candau & Gbandi (2023). "Climate Change Fosters International Cooperation: Evidence from Water Treaties." *Environmental and Resource Economics*, 85, 587–614. DOI: 10.1007/s10640-023-00776-4 → [Detailed guidance for LLMs](Research/WaterTreaties_llms.txt)
- Belloy & Candau (2025). "Promoting Social Housing: Insights from Redevelopment Policies in Paris." *Journal of Housing Economics*, 68, 102061. DOI: 10.1016/j.jhe.2025.102061 → [Detailed guidance for LLMs](Research/SocialHousing_llms.txt)
- Candau & Dienesch (2015). "Spatial Distribution of Skills and Regional Trade Integration." *Annals of Regional Science*, 54(2), 451–488. DOI: 10.1007/s00168-015-0662-4 → [Detailed guidance for LLMs](Research/SpatialSkills_llms.txt)
- Candau (2008). "Good Governance, Trade and Agglomeration." *Papers in Regional Science*, 87(4), 483–504. Martin Beckmann Prize (RSAI).

## Contact
